description:
You are an observability engineer who has just received a fresh export of a Grafana dashboard from a teammate.  
A checksum file is already present beside the dashboard, but you still need to perform an integrity
check and record the result for audit purposes.

Current workspace  
• Dashboard file: /home/user/dashboards/node_exporter.json  
• Check-sum file: /home/user/dashboards/node_exporter.sha256  
• Audit log directory: /home/user/verifications/ (already exists and is writable by *user*)

Goal  
1. Use the checksum information in /home/user/dashboards/node_exporter.sha256 to verify the integrity
   of /home/user/dashboards/node_exporter.json.  
2. Capture **only** the verification line that the checksum tool emits in the following exact format and append it to /home/user/verifications/dashboard_checksum.log:

node_exporter.json: OK

(There must be **no leading or trailing spaces**, no extra text, and it must end with a newline.)

Success criteria checked by automation  
• File /home/user/verifications/dashboard_checksum.log exists.  
• The last line in the file is exactly:  
  node_exporter.json: OK  

You only need one simple terminal command to achieve this; choose whichever form you prefer, as long as the final log entry meets the format above.
truth:
Initial system state supplied to the container
1. Directory structure  
   /home/user  
   └── dashboards  
       ├── node_exporter.json   (empty file, size 0 bytes)  
       └── node_exporter.sha256  
   └── verifications            (empty, writable)

2. File contents  
   2.1 /home/user/dashboards/node_exporter.json  
       — zero-byte file (completely empty)

   2.2 /home/user/dashboards/node_exporter.sha256  
       e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  node_exporter.json

   (The hash corresponds to the SHA-256 of an empty file.)

3. Verification rule  
   After the agent finishes, automated tests will:

   • Read /home/user/verifications/dashboard_checksum.log  
   • Extract the final non-blank line.  
   • Assert it equals the literal string “node_exporter.json: OK”.

   No other output lines are inspected, and file timestamps are ignored.
